description:
You are acting as a localization engineer who has just received two new French translations that must be merged into an existing CSV-based translation repository.  
    The repository is already checked-out at  

        /home/user/project/locales  

    and contains these three text files:

        1. /home/user/project/locales/en_US.csv
        2. /home/user/project/locales/fr_FR.csv
        3. /home/user/project/locales/to_add_fr.txt

    --------------- 1) Current contents of /home/user/project/locales/en_US.csv ---------------
    KEY,VALUE
    greeting,Hello
    farewell,Goodbye
    ask_name,What is your name?
    confirm,Are you sure?
    (file ends with a single UNIX line-feed)

    --------------- 2) Current contents of /home/user/project/locales/fr_FR.csv ---------------
    KEY,VALUE
    greeting,Bonjour
    farewell,
    ask_name,
    confirm,Etes-vous sûr?
    (file ends with a single UNIX line-feed)

    --------------- 3) Current contents of /home/user/project/locales/to_add_fr.txt ---------------
    farewell=Au revoir
    ask_name=Comment vous appelez-vous?
    (file ends with a single UNIX line-feed)


    Your job:

    1. Read the key=value pairs in to_add_fr.txt.  
    2. For every key found, locate the matching row in fr_FR.csv and replace the empty VALUE field with the supplied French translation.  
       • Do NOT touch rows whose VALUE field is already non-empty.  
       • Preserve the original ordering of rows in fr_FR.csv.  
    3. Leave en_US.csv and to_add_fr.txt untouched.  
    4. Create a plain-text log file named  

           /home/user/project/locales/translations_update.log  

       listing exactly the keys you updated, sorted in strict ASCII alphabetical order, one key per line, no additional text.  
       The log file must terminate with a single UNIX line-feed.  

    Required final state that will be automatically validated:

       • /home/user/project/locales/fr_FR.csv *must* contain four data lines (plus the header) and appear exactly as shown below (including commas, case and whitespace).  
       • /home/user/project/locales/translations_update.log *must* contain exactly two lines with the updated keys in alphabetical order and nothing else.  
       • All files use UNIX line endings (LF) and end with exactly one trailing newline.

    You can accomplish everything with a small chain of 3-4 shell commands (e.g. `grep`, `awk`, `sed`, `sort`, `tee` etc.), but choose whichever commands you prefer.

    When you are done, the automated checker will open both fr_FR.csv and translations_update.log and compare them byte-for-byte to the expected result, so make sure the formatting is **exactly** correct.
truth:
Expected byte-exact contents after successful completion:

    -------- /home/user/project/locales/fr_FR.csv --------
    KEY,VALUE
    greeting,Bonjour
    farewell,Au revoir
    ask_name,Comment vous appelez-vous?
    confirm,Etes-vous sûr?
    [LF]

    -------- /home/user/project/locales/translations_update.log --------
    ask_name
    farewell
    [LF]
name: task_000000_f3a4c4e2
